fields.label: Base box
fields.help: Base OS image pulled from Vagrant Cloud. Windows boxes require the WinRM communicator.

fields.label: Box URL (custom)
fields.help: Override the Vagrant Cloud source with a direct URL to a .box file. Leave blank to use Vagrant Cloud.

fields.label: Box version constraint
fields.help: Semantic version constraint e.g. '~> 20240101.0', '>= 2.0, < 3.0'. Leave blank for latest.

fields.label: Check for box updates
fields.help: Vagrant checks for newer box versions on every 'vagrant up'. Disable to speed up startup in CI environments.

fields.label: VM name
fields.help: Logical name used by Vagrant. Shown in 'vagrant status' and used with 'vagrant ssh <name>'.

fields.label: Hostname
fields.help: Sets /etc/hostname inside the VM. Useful when the VM needs a resolvable hostname on the local network.

fields.label: Communicator
fields.help: How Vagrant connects to the VM. Use 'ssh' for Linux/macOS guests. Use 'winrm' for Windows guests. 'none' disables communication (useful for VMs that configure themselves).

fields.label: Boot timeout (seconds)
fields.help: How long to wait for the VM to boot before declaring failure. Increase for slow machines or large boxes.

fields.label: Graceful halt timeout (seconds)
fields.help: Time allowed for 'vagrant halt' before Vagrant forces power off.

fields.label: Post-up message
fields.placeholder:
VM is ready!
Access the app at http://localhost:8080
fields.help: Message displayed to the user after 'vagrant up' completes successfully.

fields.label: Guest port
fields.help: Port inside the VM.

fields.label: Host port
fields.help: Port on your host machine.

fields.label: Host IP binding
fields.help: Bind the host port to a specific interface. Leave blank to bind on all interfaces (0.0.0.0).

fields.label: Auto-correct conflicts
fields.help: If the host port is already in use, Vagrant picks a different one automatically.

fields.label: Port identifier
fields.help: Optional label for this rule. Useful for identifying specific rules in 'vagrant port' output.

fields.label: Type
fields.help: Static assigns a fixed IP. DHCP lets VirtualBox assign an IP from its host-only DHCP pool.

fields.label: IP address
fields.help: Required when type is static. Must be in the 192.168.56.x range for VirtualBox host-only networking.

fields.label: Auto-configure interface
fields.help: Vagrant configures the network interface inside the guest automatically. Disable if you manage network config manually inside the VM.

fields.label: NIC type (VirtualBox)
fields.help: Leave blank to use VirtualBox default (82540EM). virtio offers better performance when guest drivers support it.

fields.label: Bridge adapter
fields.help: Host network adapter to bridge. Leave blank to prompt interactively on 'vagrant up'. On macOS use 'en0: Wi-Fi'. On Linux use 'eth0' or 'ens3'.

fields.label: Static IP address
fields.help: Assign a static IP to the bridged interface. Leave blank to use DHCP.

fields.label: Use DHCP-assigned default route
fields.help: When enabled, the VM's default route is set to the one provided by the bridged DHCP server. Use when the VM needs to reach external networks via the bridged interface.

fields.label: Host path
fields.help: Path on your host machine. '.' means the same directory as your Vagrantfile.

fields.label: Guest path
fields.help: Absolute path inside the VM where the folder is mounted.

fields.label: Sync type
fields.help: VirtualBox shared folders are the default and require Guest Additions. NFS is faster on macOS/Linux hosts. SMB works well on Windows. rsync is one-directional (host→guest) and works over SSH.

fields.label: Mount options
fields.help: Comma-separated mount options passed to the underlying mount command. Common: dmode=775,fmode=664 for permission control.

fields.label: rsync auto-sync (rsync type)
fields.help: When enabled, 'vagrant rsync-auto' watches for file changes and re-syncs automatically. Only applies to rsync type.

fields.label: rsync exclude patterns
fields.help: Comma-separated patterns to exclude from rsync. Only applies to rsync type.

fields.label: Disabled
fields.help: Disable this synced folder without removing it from the Vagrantfile. Useful for temporarily turning off a slow sync.

fields.label: VirtualBox VM name
fields.help: Name shown in the VirtualBox Manager UI. Defaults to VM name if not set.

fields.label: Show GUI window
fields.help: Open the VirtualBox GUI window when the VM starts. Useful for debugging boot failures.

fields.label: Linked clone
fields.help: Creates a linked clone sharing storage with the base box. Faster to create and uses much less disk space. Not compatible with some box types.

fields.label: Check Guest Additions version
fields.help: Warn if Guest Additions version does not match VirtualBox version. Disable if using the vbguest plugin to manage this automatically.

fields.label: NAT DNS host resolver
fields.help: Routes DNS queries from the VM through the host resolver. Fixes DNS issues in NAT networking. Recommended: on.

fields.label: Enable I/O APIC
fields.help: Required for multi-CPU VMs. Leave enabled.

fields.label: Nested virtualization
fields.help: Allow the VM to run hypervisors (e.g. Docker, KVM). Requires a CPU with nested virt support. Significantly impacts host performance.

fields.label: Clipboard sharing
fields.help: Share clipboard between host and VM. Only useful when GUI is enabled.

fields.label: Custom VBoxManage flags
fields.help: One VBoxManage customization per line, in Ruby array format. Each line becomes: vb.customize <line>. Full list at: https://www.virtualbox.org/manual/ch08.html

fields.label: Nested virtualization (vhv.enable)
fields.help: Enable hardware-assisted virtualization inside the VM. Required for running Docker or KVM inside VMware.

fields.label: Custom VMX settings
fields.help: Raw VMX key = value pairs, one per line. Applied directly to the VM's .vmx config file.

fields.label: Max dynamic memory (MB)
fields.help: Upper bound for Hyper-V dynamic memory. Leave blank to use static memory allocation.

fields.label: Auto-start action
fields.help: What Hyper-V does with this VM when the host boots.

fields.label: Enable nested virtualization
fields.help: Required to run Docker or other hypervisors inside the VM on Hyper-V.

fields.label: Auto-update Parallels Tools
fields.help: Automatically update Parallels Tools on every 'vagrant up'. Adds boot time but keeps tools in sync.

fields.label: Custom prlctl flags
fields.help: One prlctl customization per line in Ruby array format.

fields.label: Provisioner name
fields.help: Optional identifier. Run specific provisioners with: vagrant provision --provision-with bootstrap

fields.label: Script source
fields.help: Inline writes the script directly into the Vagrantfile. Path references an external file on the host.

fields.label: Inline script
fields.placeholder:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y nginx
fields.help: Shell script to run inside the VM. Always start with a shebang. 'set -e' stops on first error.

fields.label: Script file path
fields.help: Path relative to the Vagrantfile directory. The file is uploaded to the VM and executed.

fields.label: Script arguments
fields.help: Arguments passed to the script. Accessible as $1, $2 etc. inside the script.

fields.label: Run as root (privileged)
fields.help: Execute with sudo. Disable if the script should run as the vagrant user.

fields.label: Reboot after completion
fields.help: Reboot the VM after the script finishes. Required when installing kernel modules or making low-level system changes.

fields.label: Run trigger
fields.help: 'once' runs on first 'vagrant up' only. 'always' runs on every 'vagrant up'. 'never' disables the provisioner (activate manually with --provision-with).

fields.label: Upload path (guest)
fields.help: Where the script file is uploaded inside the VM before execution. Defaults to /tmp/vagrant-shell.

fields.label: Environment variables
fields.help: KEY=VALUE format, one per line. Injected into the shell environment before the script runs.

fields.label: PowerShell elevated (Windows)
fields.help: Run the PowerShell script with elevated UAC privileges. Windows guests only.

fields.label: Ansible type
fields.help: 'ansible' runs Ansible on the host machine. 'ansible_local' installs and runs Ansible inside the VM — no Ansible required on the host.

fields.label: Playbook path
fields.help: Path to playbook relative to the Vagrantfile.

fields.label: Inventory path
fields.help: Path to a static inventory file. Leave blank to use Vagrant's auto-generated inventory.

fields.label: Verbose level
fields.help: Ansible verbosity level. Empty = default. vvvv shows full connection debug output.

fields.label: Run only these tags
fields.help: Comma-separated Ansible tags. Only tasks matching these tags will run.

fields.label: Skip these tags
fields.help: Comma-separated tags to skip.

fields.label: Galaxy requirements file
fields.help: Vagrant will run 'ansible-galaxy install' with this file before running the playbook.

fields.label: Raw ansible-playbook arguments
fields.help: Extra arguments passed directly to ansible-playbook. Useful for --check (dry run) or --diff.

fields.label: Extra variables
fields.help: YAML key: value pairs one per line. Passed as --extra-vars to ansible-playbook.

fields.label: Source (host)
fields.help: Path on the host machine, relative to the Vagrantfile. Can be a file or directory.

fields.label: Destination (guest)
fields.help: Absolute path inside the VM. Files are uploaded as the SSH user (vagrant), not root. Use a shell provisioner after this to move files to privileged locations.

fields.label: Install Docker engine automatically
fields.help: Vagrant installs Docker CE inside the VM if not already present.

fields.label: Docker version
fields.help: Specific Docker version to install. Leave blank for latest.

fields.label: Images to pull
fields.help: One image per line. Pulled after Docker is installed.

fields.label: Build context directory
fields.help: Directory inside the VM containing a Dockerfile. Vagrant will run 'docker build' in this directory.

fields.label: Image to run
fields.help: Start a container from this image after provisioning.

fields.label: Container run arguments
fields.help: Arguments passed to 'docker run'. Include port bindings, volume mounts, env vars etc.

fields.label: Compose file path
fields.help: Run docker compose up on this file after provisioning.

fields.label: Puppet type
fields.help: 'puppet' runs Puppet Apply locally (agentless). 'puppet_server' connects to a Puppet Master.

fields.label: Manifests path
fields.help: Directory containing .pp manifest files, relative to Vagrantfile.

fields.label: Manifest file
fields.help: Entry point manifest file within the manifests directory.

fields.label: Modules path
fields.help: Directory containing Puppet modules, relative to Vagrantfile.

fields.label: Puppet Master hostname
fields.help: Required when type is 'puppet_server'.

fields.label: Chef type
fields.help: 'chef_solo' and 'chef_zero' run without a Chef Server. 'chef_client' connects to a Chef Server.

fields.label: Cookbooks path
fields.help: Path to cookbooks directory, relative to Vagrantfile.

fields.label: Run list
fields.help: One recipe or role per line. Format: recipe[cookbook::recipe] or role[rolename].

fields.label: Chef Server URL
fields.help: Required when type is 'chef_client'.

fields.label: Timing
fields.help: 'before' runs before the command. 'after' runs after.

fields.label: Vagrant command
fields.help: The vagrant command that fires this trigger.

fields.label: Info message
fields.help: Message printed to the console when the trigger fires.

fields.label: Shell command to run
fields.help: Command executed on the HOST machine (not inside the VM) when the trigger fires.

fields.label: Script path to run
fields.help: Path to a script on the host to execute when the trigger fires.

fields.label: Plugin name
fields.help: Vagrant plugin name as listed on RubyGems. Common: vagrant-vbguest (VirtualBox Guest Additions), vagrant-hostmanager (hosts file), vagrant-disksize (disk sizing).

fields.label: Version constraint
fields.help: Semantic version constraint. Leave blank for latest.

fields.label: Machine name
fields.help: Unique name for this machine. Used with 'vagrant ssh web', 'vagrant up web' etc.

fields.label: Primary machine
fields.help: The primary machine is the default target for 'vagrant ssh' and 'vagrant up' when no machine name is specified.

fields.label: Autostart
fields.help: When disabled, this machine is not started by 'vagrant up'. Must be started explicitly with 'vagrant up <name>'.

fields.label: SSH password
fields.help: Only used when key-based auth is unavailable. Not recommended for production-like environments.

fields.label: Private key path
fields.help: Path to an additional SSH private key on the host. Vagrant always tries its default insecure key first.

fields.label: SSH host override
fields.help: Override the host Vagrant connects to. Useful when using a jump host or external IP.

fields.label: Insert generated key
fields.help: Vagrant replaces the default insecure key with a generated key pair on first boot. Recommended: enabled.

fields.label: Forward SSH agent
fields.help: Forward the host SSH agent to the VM. Allows the VM to use host SSH keys for e.g. git operations without copying keys into the VM.

fields.label: Forward X11
fields.help: Enable X11 forwarding for running GUI apps inside the VM on your host display.

fields.label: Keep-alive
fields.help: Send keep-alive packets to prevent the SSH connection from dropping during long-running provisioners.

fields.label: SSH shell
fields.help: Shell used to execute remote commands. Change if the default user shell is not bash.
